blocks.text: The wiper/washer switch provides input to the MICU in response to switch operation.

The front wiper motor/rear wiper motor is controlled by the MICU. Each motor has an auto-stop contact that feeds back the motor stop position to the MICU. If the motor becomes hot due to excessive load, etc., a circuit breaker inside the motor temporarily shuts down the circuit to stop the motor.
The washer motor uses a front-rear integrated motor. A differential pressure valve is used to switch between front and rear by changing the direction of motor rotation.
